title: VimFu — gd Go to Local Definition
description: gd jumps to the local definition of the word under the cursor. It searches from the top of the current function for the first occurrence.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim
Create demo file with local variable usage

setup.content:
# Calculator

def compute_total(items):
    tax_rate = 0.08
    subtotal = 0
    for item in items:
        price = item['price']
        qty = item['qty']
        subtotal += price * qty
    tax = subtotal * tax_rate
    total = subtotal + tax
    return total

def format_receipt(items):
    lines = []
    for item in items:
        name = item['name']
        cost = item['price'] * item['qty']
        lines.append(f"{name}: ${cost}")
    return '\n'.join(lines)

steps.say: G D jumps to the local definition of the word under the cursor. Vim searches backward from the cursor for the first occurrence.

steps.comment: --- Demo 1: jump to definition of tax_rate ---

steps.say: Go to line ten where tax rate is used.
G D. Jump to where tax rate is defined.
Line four. That's where tax rate is first assigned.

steps.comment: --- Demo 2: jump to definition of subtotal ---

steps.say: Go to line eleven where total uses subtotal.
Line five. Where subtotal is first defined.
G D. Jump to the local definition. It uses simple text search, not a language server, so it works in any file type.
